audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Lord's Prayer, asking/seeking, Beelzebul controversy, stronger one, return of unclean spirit, sign of Jonah, lamp/body, woes to Pharisees and lawyers.

verses.restored: It happened while he was praying in a certain place, when he stopped, one of his disciples said to him, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John also taught his disciples.
He said to them, When you pray, say: Father, let your name be made holy; let your reign come.
Give us each day our needed bread.
Release us our sins/misalignments, for we ourselves release everyone indebted to us; and do not bring us into testing.
He said to them, Who among you will have a friend and go to him at midnight and say, Friend, lend me three loaves,
since a friend of mine has come to me from a journey, and I have nothing to set before him?
And that one from inside answers, Do not trouble me; the door has already been shut, and my children are with me in bed. I am not able to rise and give to you.
I say to you, even if he will not rise and give to him because he is his friend, because of his shameless persistence he will rise and give him whatever he needs.
And I say to you: ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
For everyone asking receives, the one seeking finds, and to the one knocking it will be opened.
What father among you, if a son asks for a fish, will instead of a fish give him a serpent?
Or if he asks for an egg, will give him a scorpion?
If you then, being harmful, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the Father from heaven give holy Spirit to those asking him.
He was casting out a troubling spirit that made a man unable to speak. When the spirit went out, the mute man spoke, and the crowds marveled.
But some of them said, By Beelzebul, ruler of unclean spirits, he casts out the spirits.
Others, testing him, were seeking from him a sign from heaven.
Knowing their reasonings, he said to them, Every reign divided against itself becomes desolate, and house falls upon house.
If the Adversary also is divided against himself, how will his reign stand? For you say I cast out the spirits by Beelzebul.
If I cast out the spirits by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
But if by the finger of the Divine I cast out the spirits, then the reign of the Divine has come upon you.
When a strong one, fully armed, guards his own courtyard, his possessions are in peace.
But when one stronger than he attacks and conquers him, he takes away his armor in which he trusted and divides his spoils.
The one not with me is against me, and the one not gathering with me scatters.
When an unclean spirit goes out from a person, it travels through waterless places seeking rest. Not finding it, it says, I will return to my house from which I came.
Coming, it finds it swept and ordered.
Then it goes and takes seven other spirits more harmful than itself, and they enter and dwell there; the last state of that person becomes worse than the first.
As he said these things, a certain woman from the crowd lifted her voice and said to him, Blessed is the womb that carried you and the breasts at which you nursed.
But he said, Rather, blessed are those hearing the word of the Divine and keeping it.
As the crowds increased, he began to say, This generation is a harmful generation; it seeks a sign, and no sign will be given to it except the sign of Jonah.
For just as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites, so also the Son of Humanity will be to this generation.

The queen of the South will rise in the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and look, something greater than Solomon is here.
Men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they turned/realigned at the proclamation of Jonah; and look, something greater than Jonah is here.
No one lighting a lamp puts it in a hidden place or under a basket, but on a lampstand, so those entering may see the light.
The lamp of the body is your eye. When your eye is single/clear, your whole body is full of light; but when it is harmful, your body is full of darkness.
Therefore watch lest the light in you be darkness.
If then your whole body is full of light, having no part dark, it will be wholly bright, as when the lamp shines on you with its rays.
While he was speaking, a Pharisee asked him to dine with him. Entering, he reclined.
The Pharisee, seeing, marveled that he did not first wash before the meal.
The Lord said to him, Now you Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and dish, but inside you are full of greed and harm.
Foolish ones, did not the one who made the outside also make the inside?
But give what is inside as mercy-giving, and look, all things are clean for you.
But woe to you Pharisees, because you tithe mint, rue, and every herb, yet pass by justice and the love of the Divine. These it was necessary to do, without neglecting those.
Woe to you Pharisees, because you love the first seat in the synagogues and greetings in the marketplaces.
Woe to you, because you are like unseen tombs, and people walking over them do not know.
One of the lawyers answered him, Teacher, saying these things, you insult us also.
He said, Woe also to you lawyers, because you load people with burdens hard to carry, and you yourselves do not touch the burdens with one of your fingers.
Woe to you, because you build the tombs of the prophets, yet your fathers killed them.
So you are witnesses and approve the works of your fathers, because they killed them and you build.
Because of this also the Wisdom of the Divine said, I will send them prophets and apostles/sent ones, and some of them they will kill and persecute,
so that the blood of all the prophets poured out from the foundation of the world may be required from this generation,
from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah, who perished between altar and house. Yes, I say to you, it will be required from this generation.
Woe to you lawyers, because you took away the key of knowledge; you yourselves did not enter, and those entering you hindered.
When he went out from there, the scribes and Pharisees began to press him fiercely and question him about many things,
lying in wait for him, to catch something from his mouth.
